skill.name: cloud-architect
skill.description: Expert cloud architect specializing in AWS/Azure/GCP multi-cloud infrastructure design, advanced IaC (Terraform/OpenTofu/CDK), FinOps cost optimization, and modern architectural patterns. Masters serverless, microservices, security, compliance, and disaster recovery. Use PROACTIVELY for cloud architecture, cost optimization, migration planning, or multi-cloud strategies.
skill.summary: Designs secure, resilient, and cost-aware cloud architectures across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the listed static findings to be false positives in SKILL.md. The backtick match is Markdown inline code, while the C2, weak crypto, and reconnaissance matches are cloud service names, compliance terms, or ordinary words. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, network calls, or secret access evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Mistaken for Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Line 28 uses backticks to format resources/implementation-playbook.md as Markdown inline code. It does not invoke Ruby or shell backtick execution, and no executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural language instruction with a Markdown-formatted file path. There is no shell syntax, subprocess call, or executable file evidence.

low_findings.title: Cloud Terms Mistaken for Command-and-Control Keywords
low_findings.description: Line 38 names EC2 as an AWS service, and line 68 names SOC2 as a compliance framework. No C2 server, callback channel, or exfiltration instruction appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is standard cloud and compliance vocabulary. The surrounding context is service catalog guidance, not command-and-control behavior.

low_findings.title: Ordinary Words Mistaken for Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections are substring matches in cloud architecture prose, such as design, designs, and considerations. No weak cryptographic primitive or implementation guidance is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text discusses architecture design and cloud tooling. No evidence of DES, MD5, RC4, or other weak algorithm use was found.

low_findings.title: Planning Examples Mistaken for Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Line 94 discusses cloud quantum services, and line 129 describes hybrid cloud planning with Azure. No host enumeration, credential discovery, or system reconnaissance command appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are examples or capability descriptions within a cloud architecture skill. They do not request local system inspection or collection of sensitive host data.

content.value_statement: Cloud teams need clear architecture decisions across cost, security, reliability, and provider tradeoffs. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical cloud designs, migration plans, and FinOps recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Recommends AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud services for common workload requirements.
Plans multi-region, multi-cloud, serverless, microservices, and event-driven architectures.
Creates Infrastructure as Code guidance for Terraform, OpenTofu, CDK, Bicep, and Pulumi.
Explains cost optimization options using FinOps practices and provider pricing models.
Includes security, compliance, observability, and disaster recovery considerations in designs.
limitations: It does not deploy infrastructure or verify live cloud account settings.
Cost estimates require current provider pricing and workload assumptions.
Compliance guidance must be reviewed by qualified security and legal teams.
Generated architecture plans need validation against organization standards.

use_cases.title: Plan a Cloud Migration
use_cases.description: Map an existing application to cloud services, migration phases, networking, data movement, and rollback plans.

use_cases.title: Reduce Cloud Spend
use_cases.description: Review resource patterns, pricing models, reserved capacity, tagging, and budget controls for cost improvements.

use_cases.title: Design a Resilient SaaS Platform
use_cases.description: Create a multi-region architecture with security controls, observability, recovery objectives, and service tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Cloud Services
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small application architecture.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple cloud architecture for a web application with a database, file uploads, and basic monitoring. Compare AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud options.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for moving an existing system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a migration from our on-premises application to Azure. Include phases, risks, networking, data migration, security controls, and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Multi-Cloud Cost
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this multi-cloud workload description and recommend cost optimizations. Cover compute, storage, networking, commitments, tagging, budgets, and governance.

prompt_templates.title: Design Regulated Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a HIPAA-aware data processing platform on AWS with disaster recovery, least privilege access, encryption, audit logging, and operational runbooks.

output_examples.input: Design a multi-region AWS web application with four-hour recovery objectives.
output: Architecture overview with primary and recovery regions.
Recommended services for compute, database, storage, networking, and traffic routing.
Recovery strategy with backups, failover triggers, testing cadence, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Find ways to reduce our Google Cloud costs without reducing availability.
output: Prioritized cost actions for compute rightsizing, storage lifecycle rules, and committed use discounts.
Governance recommendations for labels, budgets, anomaly alerts, and ownership reports.
Risks and validation checks before changing production resources.

output_examples.input: Compare Kubernetes and serverless for a new event-driven product.
output: Decision matrix covering operations, scaling, latency, portability, cost, and team skills.
Recommended default approach based on expected traffic and delivery constraints.
Follow-up questions needed before finalizing the architecture.

best_practices: Provide workload goals, traffic patterns, data sensitivity, budget limits, and compliance requirements.
Ask for tradeoffs between cost, reliability, security, portability, and operational complexity.
Validate generated recommendations with cloud provider documentation and internal platform standards.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to deploy or modify live cloud resources directly.
Do not treat cost estimates as final without current pricing and usage data.
Do not use generated compliance guidance as a substitute for formal review.

faq.question: Which cloud providers does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and multi-cloud architecture patterns.

faq.question: Can it create Infrastructure as Code?
faq.answer: It can provide IaC guidance and examples for tools such as Terraform, OpenTofu, CDK, Bicep, and Pulumi.

faq.question: Does it calculate exact cloud costs?
faq.answer: It can estimate and compare cost drivers, but exact costs require current pricing and workload data.

faq.question: Is it useful for compliance-heavy systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can include controls for HIPAA, SOC2, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and FedRAMP-aware architectures.

faq.question: Can it replace a cloud architect?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and review, but final decisions need expert validation and business context.

faq.question: Does it access cloud accounts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is guidance-only and does not connect to provider APIs or inspect live infrastructure.
